slides.title: Languages and Communication
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 8 English, Unit 1: Languages and Communication. Welcome to Unit 1 of your Year 8 English course at Ehel Academy. In this unit you will explore the powerful theme of languages and communication - how people share ideas, how rumours spread, and how the truth can be lost or found depending on how carefully we listen and speak. You will read a compelling story set in Mogadishu, learn important grammar structures, build a rich vocabulary, and practise all four... In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Summarise the central ideas and important details in Languages and Communication texts.
Support inferences and comparisons with relevant text evidence.
Use Grade 8 vocabulary accurately in discussion and writing.
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to summarise the central ideas and important details in languages and communication texts.. you will also support inferences and comparisons with relevant text evidence.. you will also use grade 8 vocabulary accurately in discussion and writing.. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: concise, barricade, zeal, longevity, definition, allot. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Languages and Communication: source text 1 and Languages and Communication: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: What Are Contrast Conjunctions?
bullets: Although, while, and whereas are called contrast conjunctions. They connect two clauses (parts of a sentence) that express different or surprising ideas....
slides.narration: The first language focus is What Are Contrast Conjunctions?. Although, while, and whereas are called contrast conjunctions. They connect two clauses (parts of a sentence) that express different or surprising ideas. They are essential tools for showing that two things can be true at the same time even when they seem to conflict. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Contrast Debate. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
